description: group of indigenous people in the Mexican state of Michoacán
description_wiki: The Purépecha (Western Highland Purepecha: P'urhepecha [pʰuˈɽepet͡ʃa]) are a group of Indigenous people centered in the northwestern region of Michoacán, Mexico, mainly in the area of the cities of Cherán and Pátzcuaro.

description: municipal seat of Zacapu, State of Michoacán, Mexico
description_wiki: Zacapu (Place of Stones), is a city and the municipal seat of the homonymous municipality.

Michoacán, formally Michoacán de Ocampo, officially the Free and Sovereign State of Michoacán de Ocampo, is one of the 31 states which, together with Mexico City, compose the Federal Entities of Mexico.
Mexico, officially the United Mexican States, is a country in North America.

description: military conflict between the United States of America and Mexico from 1846 to 1848
description_wiki: The Mexican–American War, also known in the United States as the Mexican War and in Mexico as the United States intervention in Mexico, (April 25, 1846 – February 2, 1848) was an invasion of Mexico by the United States.

surface_forms: Texas, which in Mexico at the time
description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Texas (  TEK-səss) is the most populous state in the Southern United States.

description: language isolate or small language family spoken by a quarter million Purépecha in the highlands of the Mexican state of Michoacán
description_wiki: Purépecha (autonym: Pʼurhépecha [pʰuˈɽepet͡ʃa] or Phorhé(pecha)), often called Tarascan (Spanish: Tarasco), a term coined by Spanish settlers that can be seen as pejorative to some, is a language isolate or small language family that is spoken by some 140,000 Purépecha in the highlands of Michoacán, Mexico.

surface_forms: Coahuila, which is a, you know, another state on the other side of Mexico
aliases: Estado Libre y Soberano de Coahuila de Zaragoza
description_wiki: Coahuila, formally Coahuila de Zaragoza, officially the Free and Sovereign State of Coahuila de Zaragoza, is one of the 31 states of Mexico.

description: municipal seat of San Juan de Sabinas, State of Coahuila, Mexico
description_wiki: Nueva Rosita is a town in the northeastern part of the state of Coahuila in northern Mexico.

The Basques are a Southwestern European ethnic group, characterised by the Basque language, a common culture, shared genetic ancestry to the ancient Vascones and Aquitanians, and are considered among the last remaining direct descendants of Neolithic European populations in Europe.

description: Native American tribe based in the United States and Mexico
description_wiki: The Kickapoo people (; Kickapoo: Kiikaapoa or Kiikaapoi; Spanish: Kikapú) are an Algonquian-speaking Native American tribe and Indigenous people in Mexico, originating in the region south of the Great Lakes.

description: several culturally related groups of indigenous peoples of the United States
description_wiki: The Apache ( ə-PATCH-ee) are several Southern Athabaskan language-speaking peoples of the Southwest, the Southern Plains and Northern Mexico.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Minnesota is a state in the Upper Midwestern region of the United States.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Wisconsin is a state in the Upper Midwest and Great Lakes regions of the United States.
